title: Roman Anthony calls himself out for ‘bad baseball’ after latest poor throw
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Red Sox outfielder Roman Anthony's throws are a concern during the team's 2-8 season start.
A poor throw from Anthony led to two runs in an 8-6 loss to the Brewers.
Boston may consider using Anthony as a designated hitter due to his fielding struggles.

title: Red Sox’ Willson Contreras warns Brewers he’ll ‘take one of them out’ if he’s hit by another pitch ​
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Red Sox's Willson Contreras was hit by a pitch for the 24th time by the Brewers, sparking tensions.
After being hit, Contreras slid aggressively into second base, tearing Brewers shortstop David Hamilton's pants.
Contreras warned he might retaliate if hit again, but the Brewers dismissed the threat as routine posturing.

title: Worcester man receives life sentence for home invasion
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Concepcion Rodriguez, 39, of Worcester, sentenced to life in prison for a 2022 Fitchburg home invasion.
Rodriguez faced additional charges, including armed assault, firearm possession, and larceny, from the 2022 incident.
Rodriguez's attorney plans to appeal, arguing he was wrongfully convicted and penalized as a habitual criminal.

title: Derrick White talks beer, being a Bostonian, and a promising Celtics playoff run
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Derrick White was present at Samuel Adams' event celebrating a new line of beers alongside other Boston sports figures.
Despite team challenges, the Boston Celtics, led by White and others, are second in the Eastern Conference with a 53-25 record.
White credits coach Joe Mazzulla's leadership for the Celtics' success, calling him the best coach in the league.

title: Mike Krzyzewski threw a subtle jab at Bill Belichick in response to new UNC hire
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: UNC hired former Nuggets coach Michael Malone as the new men's basketball coach, replacing Hubert Davis.
Mike Krzyzewski commented on the challenges Bill Belichick faces transitioning from the NFL to college coaching.
Belichick aims to make UNC football resemble the NFL, despite UNC's 4-8 record in his first season.

title: Person stabs dog in Milton during alleged attack
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A person stabbed a dog during an alleged attack by the animal in Milton, according to State Police.
The incident occurred on Blue Hill River Road near a local park and recreation area.
State Police and Milton Animal Control are investigating the stabbing; the dog was taken to a local vet.

title: Alex Cora reshuffles Red Sox lineup, drops Trevor Story out of No. 2 spot
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Trevor Story has been moved to No. 5 in the lineup after batting .119 with 17 strikeouts in 42 at-bats.
Masataka Yoshida is now batting second, with Willson Contreras and Wilyer Abreu also moving up the batting order.
The Red Sox have a 2-7 record, averaging 3.3 runs per game and ranking low in walk percentage and high in strikeouts.

title: Cleveland radio host says Cavaliers are ‘scared’ of facing the Celtics in the playoffs
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: ESPN Cleveland's Andrew Goldhammer claims the Cavaliers prefer the fourth playoff seed to avoid facing Boston.
The Celtics are currently second in the standings, having won 10 of their last 12 games.
Cleveland is one game behind the Knicks for the third seed, with key players recovering from injuries.

title: Where you live in Mass. can shape your electric bill — is yours getting out of control?
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts has the highest electricity costs in the continental U.S., with costs varying by community.
Customers of municipal utilities often pay half as much as those with larger investor-owned companies.
Switching to municipal aggregation allows towns to purchase electricity in bulk while using existing infrastructure.

title: Northeastern students rally over lack of communication after recent violence
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Northeastern students protested over communication failures following several violent incidents near campus, including a fatal police shooting.
Students criticized the university's delayed and limited alerts about incidents, relying instead on police updates and news sources.
University pledges increased patrols; students demand broader alerts for on- and off-campus threats to improve safety communication.
